item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a fun article, but a little short. I think some more work could be put into the description and the interview logs to make them longer, and maybe a little funnier. Maybe give it a recovery story. Also, the way that D-0672 dies is kind of cheap, and doesn't really fit with how the genie misinterprets the wish. He usually cheaps them out, but this one actually made his muscle bigger than before, and then he just dies. I don't think he has to die, but maybe he looses control of the arm. (Because only the muscle cells regenerate, not the nerve cells). Also, I think that you could add a little more substance to each of the experiments, as the article in general is kind of short. Perhaps you could include a more comprehensive transcript of an experiment, as in the description you describe what the Genie says, but then you don't have him saying it, which could be funny. Like, you describe it one way in the description, but then when the Genie actually says it it's way more over-the-top or manipulative.
No vote currently, but I do like the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a repeated paragraph (when the genie retreats and says "no refunds") but its a significant enough delete I don't want to edit it myself incase it was somehow intentional/misunderstood (also, within the first one it says "If the subject t attempts"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, super nice! +1 so hard.

I wasn't even aware that changes were made or there was a response to this post. Sorry it took so long to come back here, in fact I only wound up back here by random SCP button.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an older article of mine, and I admit it needs work. Suggested changes have been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read it all, not understanding how… the grammatical mistakes are allowed to pass.

Any personnel who attempt to gain unauthorized access to SCP-4035 will be subject to disciplinary action.

Redundant?

Once SCP-4035 has vanished, the lightbulb within SCP-4035 will lose power.

It is never mentioned that the lightbulb is powered or lit, only inserted.

Resulting Ability: Once SCP-4035-1 what manifested, D-0672 was shot twice in the chest by onsite security.

First, what what?
Second, double tap the D-Class in the chest? Even if this hack job genie did grant "muscle regeneration", that is most definitely not "organ regeneration" "bone regeneration" or any other type of regeneration, so yeah, best case scenario his muscles heal over and he still dies from internal bleeding. But also this is series 5 right? Aren't we supposed to be past the whole "we do evil s*t for science cause… science!" stuff?
I can go with the concept of a budget wish granter but this needs work. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Giuseppe Krakowski

Is this a motherfucking JonTron reference?
Even besides this, I enjoyed this skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was trying to go less of a straight up malicious angle and more of a sleazy businessman or a used car salesman angle, but I can understand not liking it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just malicious genie played straight up. there aren't really real twist or hook to this, and the examples while funny don't really convince me. 'fraid it's a -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops, must have missed those. Cleared them up and fixed the spelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, lets address the elephant in the room; all the SCP-XXXX instances.

I'd separate 4035 and 4035-1 into smaller but separate descriptions to allow for easier comprehension.

'aperate' - spelled: Apparate

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's a genie but from a real lamp instead of some old golden thing from the movies, and it messes with people?

Sounds pretty cool to me. You forgot a few XXXX's in your description and procedures, otherwise I didnt notice anything wrong. Love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Genies and car salesmen always seemed similar to me. Big thanks to the fellas in the Site19 chat who gave me more suggestions for bad superpowers. If you think of any other bad superpowers, there’s a testing log for that as well.

Other Articles of Mine
